name: linkerd-patterns
description: Implement Linkerd service mesh patterns for lightweight, security-focused service mesh deployments. Use when setting up Linkerd, configuring traffic policies, or implementing zero-trust networking with minimal overhead.
capabilities: Install Linkerd control plane and data plane proxies
Configure automatic mTLS for pod-to-pod encryption
Create ServiceProfiles for per-route metrics, retries, and timeouts
Set up TrafficSplit for canary deployments and A/B testing
Define ServerAuthorization policies for mesh TLS access control
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides Linkerd service mesh documentation and YAML templates. The static findings are false positives: shell commands are example documentation blocks, URLs point to official Linkerd resources, and the pipe-to-shell pattern is the standard installation method from Linkerd's official docs. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: This skill provides production-ready patterns for deploying and configuring Linkerd service mesh on Kubernetes, enabling automatic mTLS, traffic management, and zero-trust security policies.
limitations: Requires existing Kubernetes cluster to apply configurations
Does not install or manage Kubernetes itself
Limited to Linkerd-specific features; not a general Kubernetes tool
Does not provide troubleshooting for non-Linkerd networking issues

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up Service Mesh
useCases.description: A DevOps engineer needs to secure microservice communications with automatic mTLS and implement traffic splitting for canary deployments.

useCases.title: Security Engineer Implementing Zero-Trust
useCases.description: A security engineer wants to enforce zero-trust network policies by restricting traffic between services using Linkerd's ServerAuthorization.

useCases.title: Platform Team Managing Multi-Cluster
useCases.description: A platform team needs to extend Linkerd across multiple Kubernetes clusters for high availability and disaster recovery.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Linkerd Installation
promptTemplates.prompt: Install Linkerd on my Kubernetes cluster and verify the installation is working correctly.

promptTemplates.title: Enable Automatic mTLS
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to enable automatic mTLS for all pods in namespace 'my-app' and explain how Linkerd handles certificate rotation.

promptTemplates.title: Configure Canary Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a TrafficSplit configuration to route 10% of traffic to 'my-service-canary' and 90% to 'my-service-stable' for canary deployment.

promptTemplates.title: Set Up Service Authorization
promptTemplates.prompt: Create ServerAuthorization and Server resources to allow only the 'frontend' service account to access 'my-service' on port 8080 with mesh TLS.

bestPractices: Always run 'linkerd check' after any configuration change to verify correctness
Enable ServiceProfiles for all services to get per-route metrics and enable retries
Start with permissive policies and tighten gradually; use Linkerd's visibility to understand traffic patterns first
antiPatterns: Skipping the pre-installation check with 'linkerd check --pre'
Applying configurations without understanding the impact on existing traffic
Setting overly broad ServerAuthorization policies that defeat zero-trust goals

faq.question: What is Linkerd?
faq.answer: Linkerd is a service mesh for Kubernetes that provides automatic mTLS, traffic management, observability, and security policies for microservice communications.

faq.question: Does Linkerd require code changes?
faq.answer: No, Linkerd works by injecting a proxy sidecar into pods. Most features work without any code changes to your applications.

faq.question: How does automatic mTLS work?
faq.answer: Linkerd automatically issues and rotates TLS certificates for all pod-to-pod communications. Services authenticate each other using mutual TLS without any configuration.

faq.question: Can I use Linkerd with ingress controllers?
faq.answer: Yes, Linkerd integrates with ingress controllers like NGINX and Traefik. You may need to mark the ingress as 'unauthenticated' in ServerAuthorization for external traffic.

faq.question: What is a ServiceProfile?
faq.answer: A ServiceProfile is a Linkerd custom resource that defines per-route metrics, retries, timeouts, and traffic splits for a service. It enables advanced traffic management features.

faq.question: How do I monitor Linkerd?
faq.answer: Install the Linkerd Viz extension for built-in dashboards. Use 'linkerd viz top' for live traffic views, 'linkerd viz stat' for metrics, and 'linkerd viz edges' for service dependencies.
